place: Bophut, Ko Samui District, Surat Thani, Thailand
title: Sea-View Three-Bedroom Pool Villa with Office - Bophut
blurb: Two villas remain in an intimate hillside village of six in Bophut, on Koh Samui’s north coast — three-bedroom-plus-office sea-view homes in warm stone and wood, each with an infinity-edge pool and panoramic Gulf views. Freehold or leasehold, with an estimated 10–17% ROI. Special July 2026 offer: one villa reduced to ฿19,500,000 (from ฿21,500,000), 90% complete and finished on sale.
detail:
Set into the hillside in the Bophut area of Koh Samui’s north coast, this is an intimate village of just six contemporary villas — four already sold, with two now available:

• Type B (Plot 4) — special July 2026 price ฿19,500,000, reduced from ฿21,500,000 (including the furniture package): 182.79 m² living / 315.66 m² total build on a 524 m² plot. This villa is 90% complete and will be finished on sale.
• Type C (Plot 6) — ฿23,500,000 including the furniture package: 215 m² living / 406 m² total build on a 776 m² plot.

Both are offered freehold or leasehold, with an estimated ROI of 10–17%.

Each is a stylish three-bedroom home with a dedicated office, wrapped in panoramic glazing that opens to a breathtaking view of the sea — light shifting from the morning sun to a pink evening sunset. The architecture is warm and natural, in stone and wood, for an atmosphere of calm and comfort: an ideal choice for those who value naturalness and a quiet connection to the landscape.

The living spaces flow out to a terrace and a private infinity-edge swimming pool that appears to spill toward the horizon, with a tropical-island design carried through to the fully furnished interiors. Every villa comes with private parking, a deep well for its own water supply, and is handed over fully furnished and ready to move into.

Bophut is one of Samui’s most established and desirable areas, a short drive from Fisherman’s Village, the beaches and the island’s dining — a setting equally suited to full-time living or a turnkey holiday home.
